items.title: GitHub Trending
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: The week of April 14, 2026 has seen an exceptionally dense wave of AI agent repository activity on GitHub. The standout newcomer is Google's Agent Development Kit (google/adk-python), which accumulated 8,200+ stars in its first two weeks, providing a production-ready framework for building multi-agent systems. Other fast-rising releases include meta-llama/llama-stack (6,400+ stars), openai/codex-cli — a terminal-native coding agent with sandboxed execution (5,800+ stars), block/goose — a local-f

items.title: New AI Model Releases and Open Source Projects in April 2026 - Fazm Blog
items.author: fazm.ai
items.snippet: April 2026 has seen a surge in open-source LLM releases, with seven major foundation models shipping in the first twelve days of the month alone. Key releases include Meta Llama 4 Scout (17B active parameters, MoE architecture), Qwen3-72B (reported as the first open model to beat GPT-4o on MMLU-Pro), Qwen3-Coder-32B with 128K context, Mistral Codestral-2-22B, and DeepSeek-V3-Base. A notable architectural trend: four of the seven April releases use mixture-of-experts (MoE) architecture, and Apach

items.title: LangChain vs CrewAI vs AutoGen: Top AI Frameworks 2026
items.author: www.agilesoftlabs.com
items.snippet: In 2026, the major AI agent frameworks have all reached new maturity milestones. LangGraph 1.0 shipped in October 2025 as the first stable major release and can now be used standalone without LangChain. LangChain's ecosystem also spawned 'Deep Agents' (github.com/langchain-ai/deepagents), a new agent harness with planning tools, filesystem backends, and subagent spawning. CrewAI OSS v1.0 launched on October 20, 2025, decoupling from LangChain as a hard dependency while maintaining interoperabili

items.title: Report: Meta developing open-source versions of upcoming AI models — SiliconANGLE
items.author: siliconangle.com
items.snippet:
Meta has been active on multiple open-source AI fronts in early 2026. The Llama 4 herd, launched in early 2025, remains a cornerstone of Meta's open-source strategy — featuring Llama 4 Scout and Llama 4 Maverick (17B active params, 128 experts, 1M context window) trained on 30+ trillion tokens with natively multimodal capabilities across text, image, and video. Both models are freely available on llama.com and Hugging Face, with broad cloud and edge silicon support.

On the research side, Meta F

items.title: open-source-ai · GitHub Topics
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: April 2026 has been one of the most active months for open-source AI model releases in recent memory. Key releases tracked on Hugging Face include Meta's Llama 4 family — notably Llama-4-Scout-17B (1.2M+ downloads, runs on a single 48GB GPU) and Llama-4-Maverick-17B — alongside Alibaba's Qwen3-72B, which became the first open model to beat GPT-4o on MMLU-Pro, and Qwen3-Coder-32B with 128K context and native tool calling. Mistral's Codestral-2-22B (Apache 2.0) and Google's Gemma-3-9b (newly opene

items.title: Amp - an AI Coding Agent by Sourcegraph
items.author: sourcegraph.com
items.snippet: Cursor has been one of the most active AI coding tools in early 2026, releasing Cursor 3.0 on April 2, 2026 — a major new interface that enables running many agents in parallel across repositories and environments (local, worktrees, cloud, and remote SSH). A follow-up patch, Cursor 3.1, landed on April 13, 2026, fixing multi-root workspace hooks, markdown parsing bugs, and thinking-block streaming issues. Earlier in March, Cursor shipped self-hosted cloud agent support (keeping code and secrets

items.title: Multiagent Systems — arxiv cs.MA current listings
items.author: arxiv.org
items.snippet: This week's arxiv activity on AI agents and LLMs reflects three broad thrusts. First, evaluation infrastructure is rapidly maturing: AgentSocialBench (2604.01487) introduces a benchmark for privacy risks in human-centered agentic social networks featuring persistent, personalized LLM agent teams, while a comprehensive review (2504.19678) taxonomizes roughly 60 benchmarks spanning reasoning, math, code generation, multimodal, and task-orchestration assessments — signaling the field is shifting fr

items.title: EU Artificial Intelligence Act — Up-to-date developments and analyses
items.author: artificialintelligenceact.eu
items.snippet: In 2026, AI regulation is advancing on multiple fronts globally. The EU AI Act is entering its most consequential phase: August 2, 2026 marks the activation of the European Commission's enforcement powers over General Purpose AI (GPAI) model providers, with fines up to €35 million or 7% of global turnover for prohibited practices. All EU Member States must also establish at least one national AI regulatory sandbox by this date, and transparency obligations for AI systems will become fully enforc
